summary: Justin Brennan teaches the fundamental structure of a rental property proforma, breaking down the universal formula: Gross Potential Income → subtract economic losses (vacancy, bad debt, loss-to-lease, concessions) → add reimbursables and other income → equals EGI → subtract operating expenses → equals NOI → subtract debt service and CapEx → equals net cash. This framework applies across all property sizes from 2-unit to 500-unit multifamily.
takeaways: Gross Potential Income less 8-10% total economic losses (1-2% bad debt/concessions, 3-7% physical vacancy) gets you to Effective Gross Income (EGI)
Add Reimbursables (RUBS): water, sewer, gas, trash, pest costs tenants pay back to offset landlord burden
Add Other Income sources: pet rent, administrative fees, late fees, parking fees, tech packages, valet trash fees
Operating expenses include: admin, advertising, insurance, contract services, repairs/maintenance, turnover make-ready, payroll, utilities, property taxes, property management fees
NOI (Net Operating Income) = EGI minus total operating expenses; represents cash return with zero debt
Final Net Cash = NOI minus mortgage payments minus capital expenditures (CapEx)
Proforma structure scales universally across all property types and sizes; complexity comes in detail execution but core formula remains constant

skill_candidates.change: Improve proforma accuracy by applying 8-10% total economic loss assumption with specific breakdown (1-2% bad debt/concessions, 3-7% physical vacancy) rather than guessing
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin provides concrete Midwest market benchmarks: 1-2% bad debt/concessions, 3-7% physical vacancy range, totaling 8-10% off gross potential income

skill_candidates.change: Stop underestimating rental income by capturing all reimbursable and ancillary revenue streams (RUBS, pet rent, admin fees, late fees, parking, tech packages, valet trash)
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes 'litany list of tons' of other income potentials specifically stating these items should be 'considered' in proforma, noting RUBs are standard in 50+ unit properties

skill_candidates.change: Create year-over-year proforma projections (Year 1, 2, 3+) to calculate IRR and cash-on-cash returns rather than analyzing single-year snapshots
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explains the proforma model progresses year-by-year to generate IRR and cash-on-cash return metrics, treating each year as a separate calculation that compounds into overall investment analysis

title: Lesson 2: Understanding The Proforma
